mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.0% within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, compressing equity multiples over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in real economic activity, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and depresses forward equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint decline of INDPRO and HOUST fails to predict negative SPX returns in more than 50% of historical instances over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where CPI exceeds 3.0%, aggressive Fed rate hikes fail to cool demand, causing real rates to spike and equity valuations to contract sharply over the following month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS rise simultaneously, invalidating the negative return prediction.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real disposable income measured by RSAFS increases consumer spending capacity, which directly boosts corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The correlation between monthly RSAFS changes and 5-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.10 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls exceed 200,000 for two consecutive months while the unemployment rate remains below 4.5%, the economy overheats, prompting market repricing of tighter financial conditions and negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS exceeds 200000 for two consecutive months AND UNRATE remains below 4.5%
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative SPX returns in more than 50% of historical instances over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production rises while housing starts fall indicates sector-specific supply chain bottlenecks that constrain corporate margins, leading to negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when INDPRO rises and HOUST falls simultaneously, invalidating the negative return prediction.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 4.0%, borrowing costs exceed the sustainable growth rate of corporate earnings, triggering a valuation multiple compression that depresses equity prices over the following month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
